Petition For Minors Compromise
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of the Pending Action for the Minor, Anthony Herrera.
Dept. 514. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

William W. Haskell, Esq. and H. Nelson Meeks, Esq. appeared with petitioner Guadalupe Johanna Lopez-Gomez, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Anthony Herrera, who was not present for this morning's proceedings.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Court Certified Spanish speaking interpreter Shannon Raintree assisted Ms. Lopez-Gomez during this morning's proceedings.
Order appointing Ms. Lopez-Gomez Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed February 13, 2019.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the Compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $453.378.00.
Attorney Fees - $113,344.50.
Medical Bills/Liens - $11,631.99.
Costs - $1,207.04.

The net balance of $327,194.47 shall be disbursed as follows:

(A) $38,816.47 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Wells Fargo Bank, Mission-Ocean Branch, located at 4648 Mission Street, San Francisco, CA 94117. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

(B) The remaining $288,378.00 shall be used to purchase an Annuity with Metropolitan Tower Life Insurance Company, with periodic payments made to the minor as set forth in Paragraph 7c(2)(b) of the Order Approving the Compromise. All annuity payments shall be placed directly into the Blocked Account.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to July 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for a Progress Report on the Deposit of Money into a Blocked Account and the purchase of the annuity with Metropolitan Tower Life Insurance Company.
